format_discipline.notes: No frontmatter. First non-empty line is the H1 title. Sidecar reading.meta.json is present and parses (word_count: 1391, estimated_read_minutes: 7, citations_used: [1,2,3,4], sections confirmed). Exactly the six required ## headings are present in the correct order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References. No additions, renames, or omissions.

length_budget.notes: 1391 words is within the standard-profile target of 1200–1600. 1391 / 200 wpm = 6.96 min ≈ 7 min, within the 6–8 min target. Both bounds pass.

citation_discipline.notes: Primary resource [1] (addyo.substack.com) is cited in at least three locations: silent-failure paragraph, code-ownership section, and In Practice paragraph. All four inline markers [1]–[4] are used in the body. Bibliography lists exactly four entries matching markers 1–4. No orphan inline markers; no orphan bibliography entries.

findings: edge cases: 'edge cases are covered in topic 11.9' — this is named-and-deferred form (bare name + topic pointer, not bolded, not defined). Confirmed acceptable by the open corpus soft finding.
All new terms introduced in this topic (golden rule, loud failure, silent failure, four-question test, accumulator pattern) are bolded and given plain-English meanings at first use.
spec-first workflow is referenced as prior knowledge from topics 11.3–11.7, which is correct — it is a prior-sequence concept.
No acronyms appear unexpanded. 'AI' is used as an everyday term consistent with prior topics in the sequence.
No hedging phrases detected. Sentence register is direct and addresses the learner as 'you' throughout.
No paragraph introduces 3+ brand-new terms simultaneously. The two-term introduction (loud failure / silent failure) in one bullet-pair block is acceptable as a contrast pair.
Concrete-first discipline maintained: essay analogy precedes the golden rule definition in Overview; code example in Worked Example is walked before any abstract conclusion is drawn.
All paragraphs are ≤4–5 sentences; no wall-of-text blocks detected.
The 'Do:' and 'Don't:' guidance in In Practice is rendered as bullet list and table respectively — correct.
Worked Example uses numbered walk-through lines with inline four-question analysis — multi-step procedure is listed, not run-on prose.
Signs-a-line-needs-investigation block is a bullet list, not prose.
Definitions use the bold-term — plain-meaning shape throughout (loud failure, silent failure, accumulator pattern).
The 'It ran without errors' vs 'I understand it' contrast is rendered as a markdown table — correct.
Inline examples appear within Key Concepts sections (variable name examples, code snippets), not deferred entirely to Worked Example.
All concepts taught fall within the topic title and the declared concept list: golden rule, loud/silent failure, four-question test, code ownership, plausible-looking wrong code.
spec-first workflow is reused, not re-taught — referenced as prior knowledge from 11.3–11.7.
edge cases appear as a one-sentence named-and-deferred pointer ('edge cases are covered in topic 11.9') — not bolded, not defined — compliant with scope_discipline mention-and-defer rules.
No scope_creep finding.

10 assertions sampled across all sections. All 10 trace directly to corpus sections: §5.2 (loud/silent failure), §5.3 (code ownership / Osmani quote), §5.4 (ran-without-errors code block), §5.5 (four questions verbatim), §5.6 (off-by-one/wrong-variable/wrong-default/missing-edge-case list).

The Worked Example adapts the corpus §5.4 code block (names changed from ['Ana','Bo','Cara'] to ['Alice','Bob','Charlotte']) but the logic, walkthrough structure, and analytical conclusion are faithful to the corpus.
0 unsupported assertions detected out of 10 sampled. Hard fail threshold is >2; well within tolerance.
No fabricated claims, no external material imported beyond what the corpus contains.

verdict: PASS. All eight checks pass. The reading meets the standard-profile bar for topic 11.8. Format is clean (no frontmatter, correct six-section template, valid sidecar). Word count (1391) and reading time (7 min) are within the 1200–1600 / 6–8 min standard-profile targets. Primary source [1] (addyo.substack.com) and all four references are properly cited with no orphan markers. The sole forward reference ('edge cases, topic 11.9') is in correct named-and-deferred form. All new terms are defined at first use; no jargon dumps; concrete-first discipline held throughout. Formatting is scannable: bullet lists, a contrast table, bold-term definitions, and a numbered worked example. Scope is clean — no scope_creep; all taught concepts belong to this topic's declared concept list. Corpus faithfulness is high: all 10 sampled assertions trace to the corpus with no unsupported claims. No assessment references (A4, due-week, weight) appear in the learner-facing prose. Against the gold-example bar (2-1, standard depth): the reading matches the key quality moves — defines before use, concrete analogy first, paragraphs ≤4 sentences, lists carry the weight, stays on the headline. The reading is approved to advance.
